docs.title: Introduction
docs.text:
Welcome to the official documentation for FindMyClient API.

The API is built for asynchronous search processing. Each search request is queued as a background job and returns a unique job_id. You can retrieve results by polling the result endpoint until the job status is marked as completed.

Both GET and POST methods are supported for flexible integration with applications, scripts, and automation tools.

docs.title: Workflow Overview
docs.text: The API follows a simple asynchronous workflow.

docs.title: 1. Submit Request
docs.text: Call /search to create a new background search job.

docs.title: 2. Receive Job ID
docs.text: The API immediately returns a unique job_id.

docs.title: 3. Check Status
docs.text: Poll /result/<job_id> periodically to monitor progress.

docs.title: 4. Completion
docs.text: Once the status becomes completed, retrieve the final search results.

docs.title: Base URL
docs.text: https://findmyclient.org/api

docs.title: POST / GET /search
docs.text: Submit a new search request.

docs.title: POST Request
docs.text:
{
  "query": "singapore cafe"
}

docs.title: GET Request
docs.text: /api/search?query=singapore+cafe

docs.title: Response
docs.text:
{
  "status": "processing",
  "job_id": "60a414d4-776b-4480-b6e1-ce541439cd51"
}
Field Type Description status string Current processing state job_id string Unique identifier for the search job

docs.title: GET /result/<job_id>
docs.text: Retrieve the current status and results of a search job.

docs.title: Example
docs.text: /api/result/60a414d4-776b-4480-b6e1-ce541439cd51

docs.title: Processing Response
docs.text:
Returned while the job is still running.

{
  "status": "processing",
  "result": null
}

docs.title: Completed Response
docs.text:
Returned once the search has completed successfully.

{
  "status": "completed",
  "result": {
    "query": "singapore cafe",
    "total_websites": 10,
    "total_emails_found": 3,
    "emails": [
      "contact@pscafe.com"
    ]
  }
}

docs.title: Introduction
docs.text:
Welcome to the official documentation for FindMyClient API.

The API is built for asynchronous search processing. Each search request is queued as a background job and returns a unique job_id. You can retrieve results by polling the result endpoint until the job status is marked as completed.

Both GET and POST methods are supported for flexible integration with applications, scripts, and automation tools.

docs.title: Workflow Overview
docs.text: The API follows a simple asynchronous workflow.

docs.title: 1. Submit Request
docs.text: Call /search to create a new background search job.

docs.title: 2. Receive Job ID
docs.text: The API immediately returns a unique job_id.

docs.title: 3. Check Status
docs.text: Poll /result/<job_id> periodically to monitor progress.

docs.title: 4. Completion
docs.text: Once the status becomes completed, retrieve the final search results.

docs.title: Limitations
docs.text: The FindMyClient API has the following limitations to ensure stable and fair usage across all users.

docs.title: Rate Limits
docs.text: Requests are subject to rate limiting. Excessive usage may result in throttling or temporary request delays. Please ensure your integration respects safe request pacing.

docs.title: Asynchronous Processing
docs.text:
All requests are processed asynchronously. After submitting a request, you must poll the result endpoint:

/result/{job_id}

to retrieve the final output once processing is complete.

docs.title: Processing Time
docs.text:
Processing time may vary depending on:

Query complexity Current system load Availability of external data sources

Some requests may complete quickly, while others may require additional time.

docs.title: Data Availability
docs.text:
Results depend on publicly available data. In some cases:

Business contact information may not exist Email addresses may not be publicly accessible Partial or limited data may be returned

docs.title: Concurrent Requests
docs.text: Concurrent request volume is limited to ensure system stability. High parallel usage may result in throttling or delayed processing.

docs.title: Resources
docs.text:
FindMyClient.org provides a real-time API for discovering public business contact information.

A community open-source version is available for local use, while the hosted API delivers fast, scalable, up-to-date results for developers and automation workflows.

Github Repo: https://github.com/Rottie420/findmyclient

docs.title: Python
docs.text:
This example demonstrates how to interact with the FindMyClient API using a simple Python script. It shows the full workflow for executing an asynchronous search request, including job creation, polling, and result retrieval.

The process works in two steps:

  1. Start a search job by sending a POST request to /search, which returns a job_id.
  2. Poll the result endpoint /result/{job_id} until the job is completed and the final data is available.

This approach ensures efficient handling of long-running searches without blocking the system, making it suitable for automation tools, CLI scripts, and backend integrations.

Once the job status is marked as completed, the script returns structured business data extracted from the API.


import requests
import time

BASE_URL = "https://findmyclient.org/api"


def search(query: str):
    # 1. Start search (POST request)
    response = requests.post(
        f"{BASE_URL}/search",
        json={"query": query},
        timeout=30
    )

    response.raise_for_status()
    data = response.json()

    job_id = data.get("job_id")
    print("Job started:", job_id)

    # 2. Poll result
    while True:
        result_url = f"{BASE_URL}/result/{job_id}"
        res = requests.get(result_url, timeout=30)
        res.raise_for_status()
        result = res.json()

        status = result.get("status")

        if status == "completed":
            return result["result"]

        print("Still processing...")
        time.sleep(2)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    output = search("singapore cafe")
    print("\nFinal Result:")
    print(output)

docs.title: cURL
docs.text:
You can interact with the FindMyClient API directly using curl for both starting a search and retrieving results.

Start a Search Job


curl -X POST "https://findmyclient.org/api/search" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "query": "singapore cafe"
  }'
